channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Autumn Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
Another week has ticked through in Forza Horizon 6, bringing around a fresh slew of content to charge through as the Horizon Decades Festival Playlist heads into Autumn.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist. Now, Horizon Decades continues with Autumn.

For all the challenges to complete, the rewards to earn, and everything else available, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

As usual, Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 drop on a daily basis, but they remain available until the end of the current season, and potentially even longer.

This means that you don’t have to make sure you log into the game daily to tick off the objectives, and can instead stack them up to create a to-do list to tick off before the end of the season.

  • Related: Forza Horizon 6 Giving All Players a Free Car After Eliminator Issues

The seven Daily Challenges for the Autumn season in the Horizon Decades Festival Playlist and the rewards they provide are outlined in the table below.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

As always, Weekly Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are themed around a specific car, which this week is the 1991 GMC Syclone.

You’ll need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must be completed in the set order, and the final reward is Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits. Like last week, you can earn the car through a Seasonal Championship if you don’t have it unlocked already.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Autumn Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own & drive the 1991 GMC Syclone.
  2. Complete 2 runs at the Irokawa Quarter Mile Drag Meet
  3. Earn 3 Awesome Wreckage Skills
  4. Win a Street Race

All Horizon Decades Autumn Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Spring Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

Check out the Horizon Decades Series trailer below.

What do you make of the rewards available this week? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at all the Wheelspin Exclusive cars in Forza Horizon 6, and don’t forget to sign up for our newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: GTA 6 Has Taken Over The PS5 Home Screen
item.content:encoded:
In case you weren’t aware, Grand Theft Auto 6 is releasing this November, and there’s an unprecedented amount of hype for the game. Pre-orders have just gone live, and fans from around the world have flocked to the digital storefronts to get their copy locked and loaded.

If, somehow, you weren’t aware of Grand Theft Auto 6’s impending arrival, PlayStation will be sure to let you know, as the console has had a marketing makeover for the game, unlike anything the platform has ever seen before.

GTA 6 Is The First Thing You See On PS5

As soon as you boot up your PlayStation 5, the first thing you’ll see is a remodelled Welcome Tab, where the GTA 6 logo will fade into view, before the store page of the game takes over the screen, as seen above. Check out the full load animation below:

Advertised as the ‘biggest, most immersive evolution of the Grand Theft Auto series yet,’ you’ll see in bold the release date—coming November 19, 2026, with pre-orders available now.

  • Related: All GTA 6 Editions, Prices, Content Included & Pre-Order Bonuses

As if that wasn’t enough, head on over to the Store Tab, and GTA 6 is plastered across the screen. Considering that the game is being marketed as best played on PS5, with PS5 Pro enhancements confirmed for the title, it makes sense that Sony would lean heavily into pushing the game onto its player base, and the movement is officially in full swing.

Even the mobile PlayStation app has received a GTA 6-inspired makeover, with the cool Miami Summer hues backing the PS logo. Sony is going all-out marketing GTA 6, and it’s especially telling that these steps aren’t even taken for its first-party titles: GTA 6 really is a different beast.

In the meantime, give yourself a reminder of the GTA 6 second trailer, as the hype continues to build.

Have you pre-ordered GTA 6 yet? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsl

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: GTA 6 Will Reportedly Have a Physical Disc Release in December 2026
item.content:encoded:
A new report has claimed that GTA 6 will have a physical disc release just one month after its initial launch.

The new report comes from ppe.pl, which has accurately reported on past physical game releases.

GTA 6 Physical Release Date

According to the outlet, GTA 6 will have a physical disc release in December 2026, despite the game originally being said to have no physical release. While ppe.pl has been shaky as of late with its leaks, it has accurately leaked other physical game release announcements, as its “source” is someone who obtains information from the backend of a retail store.

The news that GTA 6’s current physical release will only include a code in the box has understandably not gone down well with the gaming community, as it sets a precedent for other game publishers.

In theory, if people are simply ‘not bothered’ by a code in the box, it could mark the end of publishers releasing games on discs, meaning that physical game media will die out.

What’s likely, though, is that Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive have made the conscious choice not to release a disc version of GTA 6 day and date to prevent leaks. While the game can be locked with a day-one patch, a disc version on launch will undoubtedly see discs being sold at a premium weeks before its intended release date.

Related: All GTA 6 Editions, Prices, Content Included & Pre-Order Bonuses

PlayStation has been marketed as the best place to play GTA 6, with practically no loading screens on the PS5, as well as an unannounced suite of PS5 Pro enhancements. 

GTA 6 is scheduled to launch on November 19, 2026, on the Xbox Series S|X and PlayStation 5.

Will you be buying GTA 6 as a physical disc release if it does indeed release in December? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: GTA 6 XBOX Files Hint At New GTA Online Multiplayer
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders are live across the world, giving you the chance to buy the game ready for its November 19 launch. With thousands of players flocking to the XBOX and PlayStation Stores to pre-order the game, some have noticed an interesting detail in the download files, and it might hint at the future of GTA Online.

PlayStation has been marketed as the best place to play GTA 6, with practically no loading screens on the PS5, as well as an unannounced suite of PS5 Pro enhancements. That said, it was XBOX fans who made the in-game file discoveries, as the future of GTA 6 multiplayer takes an interesting turn.

GTA Online Seems More & More Likely

Thanks to X user GlowDevs, a closer look at the GTA 6 XBOX files has been shared, as the game is seemingly broken down into a host of Add-ons.

A lot is run-of-the-mill stuff, with the Ultimate Edition bonuses and Vintage Vice City Pack included as separate downloads, but what’s caught the attention of the fanbase is the final Add-on, simply titled ‘Story.’ Captioned below, it mentions that it ‘doesn’t need to be installed.’

As you can imagine, that’s caused a stir in the community, as up until now, the game has been marketed as a ‘single-player experience,’ with no sign of GTA Online in sight. But if there was no other content in GTA 6, like an online mode, why would you be able to not download the Story content?

  • Related: All GTA 6 Editions, Prices, Content Included & Pre-Order Bonuses

It’s led many to believe that we’ll see a similar situation with GTA 6 as we have with previous Rockstar titles. With Red Dead Redemption 2 and GTA 5, you could choose which elements of the game you wanted to download, with the single-player and online modes being separate installs. Could we see the same with GTA 6? It’s looking increasingly likely.

As things stand, though, Rockstar remains tight-lipped on the future of GTA Online. Until we get official confirmation, it’s best to temper expectations, even if everyone expects GTA 6 to get some form of multiplayer in the future.

In the meantime, give yourself a reminder of the GTA 6 second trailer, as the hype continues to build.

Would you play the next iteration of GTA Online? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 25, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Toxic substance
  • Hint 2: Not poison
  • Hint 3: One of Spider-Man’s biggest villains

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Viper
  • Deadly
  • Spider
  • Toxin
  • Snake
  • Scorpion
  • Poison
  • Poisonous
  • Venomous

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 25, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Venom

Venom is a toxic substance produced by an animal and actively injected into another animal through a wound, like a bite or sting.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 26.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite June 25 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite continues to roll out new content across various game modes, and the servers are unavailable on June 25 as Epic Games deploys additional content.

Following on from the shift to Chapter 7 Season 3, Fortnite is set for the first major patch of the season, which will also bring a host of new experiences and content into modes outside of the main Battle Royale.

If you’re wondering when you can dive back into Fortnite today, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the June 25 update, though the exact time of their return has not been confirmed by Epic Games.

While the servers went off earlier for the last patch on June 6, going down immediately after the conclusion of the live event, Epic Games will return to the familiar downtime schedule for the first mid-season update.

  • Related: Fortnite Adding Five New Sprites & Soccer Stadium in Next Update

Fortnite’s servers are set to go offline at around 4 AM ET on June 25 and will remain unavailable for around three-to-four hours, based on the standard downtime for updates of this size.

That means you can expect the Fortnite servers to return at around 7-8 AM ET on June 25, but social media is the best place to track when the servers are back online.

What’s in The Fortnite June 25 Update?

The headline addition in the June 25 update in Fortnite is the start of Season 9 in Fortnite OG, which is expected to culminate with a live event ahead of Season X.

The seasonal shift in Fortnite OG comes alongside a new Battle Pass, updates to the map and POIs, and loot pool updates, but it’s not just Fortnite OG that’s seeing fresh additions.

  • Related: Fortnite Players Are Spending Real Money to Fast-Track Sprite Collecting

In the main Battle Royale, five new Sprites are being added to the loot pool to collect, including a World Cup-themed soccer Sprite.

The update should also drop fresh hints on what’s coming to Fortnite in the future, while it also marks the start of Fortnite’s Gone Wild event, which runs through July 9.

Check out the latest Fortnite video below, showcasing the Supergirl Skin and Krypto Sidekick.

What do you want to see added to Fortnite this season? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next, take a look at all the Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses for GTA 6, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Xbox Employees Warn Layoffs Could Be Used for Retaliation
item.content:encoded:
As reports of another set of mass layoffs at Xbox continue to make the rounds, several former employees are warning current workers to be prepared, claiming previous workforce reductions were sometimes used to target employees who reported workplace issues or challenged management.

The concerns were detailed in a report from Game Developer, which spoke with four former Xbox employees who described what they say was a recurring pattern across Xbox-owned studios. According to Game Developer’s sources, employees who filed complaints with human resources, served as witnesses in workplace investigations, or spoke out against alleged toxic behavior often found themselves laid off, placed on performance improvement plans, or pushed toward leaving the company.

Xbox HR Ignored Complaints

Among those speaking publicly is former Halo Studios art director Glenn Israel, who has previously accused studio leadership of harassment and retaliation. Israel told the publication that employees who have raised concerns internally should begin preserving documentation ahead of any future layoffs.

“Redundancy will be considered a retaliatory action,” Israel said while advising employees to keep records of communications and complaints.

Israel also urged workers to understand their legal rights if they are impacted by future cuts.

“If you are laid off, consult an attorney before signing anything and at the very least secure a document retention agreement,” he said.

The report comes as Xbox is set to lay off a number of employees as well as shutter multiple studios. While the company hasn’t publicly announced additional cuts, reports have suggested more restructuring could be on the horizon.

  • Ghost Recon Struggles | Project OVR Scope Cut To Meet Deadlines

Israel alleges he experienced retaliation firsthand. During his time at Halo Studios, he says he simultaneously filled three director-level positions across multiple projects and was recognized by Xbox with a special service award for the work. Despite that, he was laid off in late 2025. According to the report, his separation agreement stated that his art director position was no longer necessary.

Israel claims another Halo Studios employee was promoted to an art director role roughly one month after his departure.

Beyond Israel’s account, several anonymous former employees described similar experiences.

One source told Game Developer they were included in a major layoff after serving as a witness in an investigation involving a studio executive who allegedly berated employees during meetings. According to the source, the executive attempted to identify those who had participated in the investigation and later held influence over layoff decisions.

Another former employee described working under a supervisor they characterized as verbally abusive. After challenging that supervisor during a meeting, the employee said they were placed on a performance improvement plan for “disrespecting” management. The source claimed that subsequent complaints to human resources were ignored.

A third former employee alleged that leadership at one Xbox-owned studio routinely retaliated against workers who pushed back on workplace conditions. The source also claimed they witnessed retaliation against an employee who sought disability accommodations.

“If you show any signs of not drinking the Kool-Aid … we don’t want you anymore,” the former employee told the publication about the actions.

Microsoft did not provide a comment to Game Developer before the report was published. Insider Gaming has also reached out for comment.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP
